title: A method for controlling a vapour compression system based on estimated flow
abstract: A method for controlling a vapour compression system (1) comprising a compressor unit (2) comprising at least two compressors (3, 12), a heat rejecting heat exchanger (4),a receiver (6), an expansion device (7) and an evaporator (8) arranged in a refrigerant path is disclosed. At least one of the compressors is a main compressor (3) being fluidly connected to an outlet of the evaporator (8) and at least one of the compressors is a receiver compressor (12) being fluidly connected to a gaseous outlet (10) of the receiver (6). A flow of vapour entering the receiver (6), such as a mass flow of vapour entering the receiver (6) is estimated and compared to a first threshold value. In the case that the estimated flow is above the first threshold value, a pressure prevailing inside the receiver (6) is controlled by operating the receiver compressor (12).
claims:
Claims 1. A method for controlling a vapour compression system (1) comprising a compressor unit (2) comprising at least two compressors (3, 12), a heat rejecting heat exchanger (4), a receiver (6), an expansion device (7) and an evaporator (8) arranged in a refrigerant path, the expansion device (7) being arranged to control a supply of refrigerant to the evaporator (8), at least one of the compressors being a main compressor (3) being fluidly connected to an outlet of the evaporator (8) and at least one of the compressors being a receiver compressor (12) being fluidly connected to a gaseous outlet (10) of the receiver (6), the method comprising the steps of: - estimating a flow of vapour entering the receiver (6), comparing the estimated flow to a first threshold value, and in the case that the estimated flow is above the first threshold value, controlling a pressure prevailing inside the receiver (6) by operating the receiver compressor (12). 2. A method according to claim 1, wherein the first threshold value is related to a minimum capacity of the receiver compressor (12) . 3. A method according to claim 1 or 2, wherein the step of estimating the flow of vapour entering the receiver (6) is performed based on a current compressor capacity of the compressor unit (2) . 4. A method according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the step of estimating the flow of vapour entering the receiver (6) is performed based on an enthalpy of refrigerant leaving heat rejecting heat exchanger (4) . 5. A method according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the vapour compression system (1) further comprises a high pressure expansion device (5, 13) arranged in the refrigerant path between an outlet of the heat rejecting heat exchanger (4) and an inlet of the receiver (6), and wherein the step of estimating the flow of vapour entering the receiver (6) is performed based on a flow through the high pressure expansion device (5, 13) . 6. A method according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the vapour compression system (1) further comprises a bypass valve (11) fluidly interconnecting the gaseous outlet 

 (10) of the receiver (6) and the main compressor (3), the method further comprising the steps of: comparing the estimated flow to a second threshold value, and in the case that the estimated flow is above the second threshold value, opening the bypass valve (11) and controlling the pressure prevailing inside the receiver (6) by operating the receiver compressor (12) and the bypass valve (11). 7. A method according to claim 6, wherein the second threshold value is related to a maximum capacity of the receiver compressor (12) . 8. A method according to any of the preceding claims, further comprising the step of, in the case that the estimated flow is below the first threshold value, maintaining the receiver compressor in a non-operating state in the case that it is not operating. 9. A method according to claim 8, wherein the vapour compression system (1) further comprises a bypass valve (11) fluidly interconnecting the gaseous outlet (10) of the receiver (6) and the main compressor (3), the method further comprising the step of controlling a pressure prevailing inside the receiver (6) by means of the bypass valve (11) . 10. A method according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the vapour compression system (1) further comprises an air condition unit having an outlet being fluidly connected to an inlet of the receiver (6), and wherein the step of estimating the flow of vapour into the receiver (6) is performed based on a load of the air condition unit.
